assertions.title: Daemon-event cross-area flow is executable with authenticated test context

steps.action: Execute daemon-event route tests with the new test-auth seam
steps.expected: Test-context daemon auth is accepted in non-production, invalid/production paths are rejected, and no X-Daemon-Token bypass is allowed
steps.observed: Route tests passed with explicit coverage for valid test-auth, invalid secret rejection, production rejection, and token precedence rejection.

steps.action: Execute live terminal daemon-event path under test auth and capture PartyKit user-channel broadcast
steps.expected: Terminal v2 daemon-event should process and publish refetch:["delivery-loop"] over websocket
steps.observed: After seeding a fresh workflow/thread for fixture user `e2e-test-user-001` and restarting a coherent local stack (`web:3100`, `broadcast:1999`) with explicit runtime vars, live run `val-cross-live-1775844336645` returned `200` ack for `event-live-1775844336645` and websocket capture observed thread patch `{ op: "refetch", refetch: ["delivery-loop"] }` for fresh thread `7c47b8e0-25bd-4e3a-82d6-6d7e8b154254` / chat `27a944bc-fbe4-45c3-8ee5-b6194b19d460`.

commands: pnpm -C apps/www exec vitest run src/app/api/daemon-event/route.test.ts
signed Better Auth fixture session cookie -> GET http://127.0.0.1:3100/api/internal/daemon-token
node websocket+POST validator script against web=http://localhost:3100, broadcast=ws://127.0.0.1:1999
commandResults: PASS: fixture daemon token minted from http://127.0.0.1:3100/api/internal/daemon-token and accepted by http://127.0.0.1:3100/api/internal/broadcast?channel=user:e2e-test-user-001
PASS: browser websocket probe stayed open on ws://127.0.0.1:1999/parties/main/user:e2e-test-user-001?apiKey=<daemon-token> and received direct PartyKit broadcast payloads
PASS: websocket payload contained {"op":"refetch","refetch":["delivery-loop"]} for fresh target thread/chat and run context advanced to completed

summary: VAL-CROSS-001 is executable and passing with live evidence: authenticated test-context daemon-event ingress completed end-to-end `/api/daemon-event` -> broadcast websocket `refetch:["delivery-loop"]` flow without weakening production auth behavior.
